key_points: One alum lived in resident halls and served as RA, positive experience
Limited specific housing complaints in available data
Previous governor threatened campus closure, creating instability concerns
key_quotes: I lived in the resident hall and served as a RA... if I could go back in time, I would do it all over again
The progression is impressive given the previous governor wanted to close the campus or consolidate with UIC

key_points: Marching band is a growing cultural centerpiece students are proud of
Sorority and Greek life present and meaningful to participants
Small campus community means social scene is limited but tight-knit
key_quotes: I am a part of the Marching Band. We are trying really hard to create a college level marching culture at Chicago
I'm a proud member of the Daughters of Issachar chapter... I received my BA and MA from CSU

key_points: No specific dining hall comments in available student data
Campus tour video focuses on academics and programs, not dining
Insufficient data to evaluate dining quality or options
key_quotes: No direct quotes about dining available from sources

key_points: Faculty strikes create student anxiety about losing instruction time
Location in South Side Chicago raises safety-adjacent stress for some
No dedicated mental health support resources mentioned by students
key_quotes: I am a Junior at Cal State Fullerton... the CSU Faculty will be striking the first week of the Spring Semester, which means... they wont be holding their class. This is genuinely terrifying
I'm a student at GSU I understand that teachers deserve more... I'm not happy teachers are treated this badly

key_points: Pell Grant reversals reported with no explanation or notice given
Financial aid office communication described as unclear and confusing
CSU system generally affordable but aid administration is problematic
key_quotes: My Pell Grant was initially credited to my account in mid August, then completely reversed just a few days later for the exact same amount. No explanation, no notice, just gone
I spoke with financial aid a month ago, everything seemed fine, but now my aid [is gone]

key_points: New doctoral programs in education and pharmacy signal academic growth
MBA program now available — not offered in earlier decades
Faculty strikes directly disrupt classes and academic continuity
key_quotes: The doctoral programs in education and pharmacy is a great step forward. I believe CSU is now offering a MBA in business now which in my era was not available
Dr. Z, the current president is a great leader, educator

key_points: Admin asked students to report striking professors — deeply adversarial move
CSU management walked out of faculty negotiations, canceling all meetings
School communication described as 'terrible' by a current student transferring out
key_quotes: My school's asking us to narc on the professors who cancel class because of it
CSU Management Walks Out of Negotiation Meeting and Cancels All Negotiation Meetings
I have been working real hard to transfer out of this school for many reasons ranging from different major options, terrible school communication, and a small lack of facilities

key_points: Located on Chicago's South Side — dismissed unfairly by outsiders but meaningful to community
Campus described as visually impressive by visitors unfamiliar with it
Previous consolidation threats with UIC suggest ongoing identity/location vulnerability
key_quotes: This is a very nice college that's located on the southside of Chicago. Wow, let the news tell it the southside has nothing to offer
The southside of Chicago will always be the MECCA for all of my family members from Arkansas that migrated here and went to college at Chicago State University
The previous governor wanted to close the campus or consolidate with UIC

key_points: Notable alumni include figures from business, politics, and entertainment
Limited employer recruiting data or career outcomes mentioned by students
Pharmacy and education doctoral programs suggest professional pipeline building
key_quotes: Oprah, Kanye West, Michael Jordan, Common, Barack and Michelle Obama... are just a few of the most famous Chicagoans [connected to the area]. They could have or should have stepped up to help this fine University
The doctoral programs in education and pharmacy is a great step forward

key_points: As a PBI serving underrepresented students, tuition is relatively accessible
Pell Grant reversals and aid confusion undercut actual affordability
Growing graduate programs add credential value for long-term investment

I received my BA and MA from CSU! If I could go back in time, I would do it all over again plus more

Underdog identity is a feature for some, a concern for others
